---
title: "Skills"
---

::: {.page-intro}
::: {.lead-text}
A concise overview of the tools and methods I use most often in analytics and machine learning work.
:::
:::

::: {.two-column-grid}
::: {.section-panel}
### Programming

<ul class="compact-list">
<li><strong>Python</strong>: Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, PyTorch, Scikit-learn</li>
<li><strong>R</strong>: Tidyverse, ggplot2</li>
<li><strong>SQL</strong>: Snowflake, PostgreSQL</li>
<li><strong>C++</strong></li>
</ul>
:::

::: {.section-panel}
### Machine Learning

<ul class="compact-list">
<li>Regression, classification, clustering</li>
<li>Decision trees, random forest, XGBoost</li>
<li>Neural networks and deep learning</li>
<li>NLP, embeddings, and LLM applications</li>
</ul>
:::

::: {.section-panel}
### Analytics & Visualization

<ul class="compact-list">
<li>Exploratory data analysis</li>
<li>Time series and network analysis</li>
<li>Data visualization</li>
<li>Dashboarding and insight communication</li>
</ul>
:::

::: {.section-panel}
### Tools

<ul class="compact-list">
<li>Git and GitHub</li>
<li>Docker</li>
<li>VS Code and Cursor</li>
<li>Excel and PowerPoint</li>
<li>macOS and Linux</li>
<li>English, Spanish, Mandarin Chinese</li>
</ul>
:::
:::
